name: PR-MU 78 Umbría del Bosque Trail
name_original: Vuelta al Morrón de la Cabra — Circular por La Umbría del Bosque (PR-MU 78)
description: Hike the moderate 9.6-mile (15.5 km) Return to Morrón de la Cabra circular route, offering diverse landscapes in Sierra Espuña.
region_intro: Hiking around Mula offers diverse landscapes, from fertile valleys and historic towns to challenging mountain paths. The region is characterized by its varied topography, including pine forests, rosemary bushes, and rocky terrain, particularly within the Sierra de Ricote. The Segura River flows through the picturesque Ricote Valley, providing tranquil walking opportunities alongside agricultural lands. Best hiking trails around Mula The most popular hiking route is Fuente Caputa and Rambla de Perea Loop, an easy 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete, offering scenic views and accessible paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is PR-MU 78 Umbría del Bosque Trail, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This route winds through diverse landscapes, including pine forests and varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Trail through pine trees – Kilometer Marker MC 3 loop from Casas Nuevas, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Mula is defined by mountain paths, pine forests, and fertile valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding treks. The routes in Mula are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 170 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Mula's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mula?
A: Mula offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 35 routes available. These trails cater to different preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks through diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mula's hiking trails?
A: Hikers in Mula will encounter a diverse mix of environments. The terrain includes fertile valleys, historic towns, pine forests, rosemary bushes, and rocky mountain paths, particularly within the Sierra de Ricote. The picturesque Ricote Valley, with the Segura River, also offers tranquil walking opportunities alongside agricultural lands.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels in Mula?
A: Yes, Mula provides trails for all skill levels. There are 11 easy routes perfect for a relaxed walk, 21 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 3 difficult hikes for experienced trekkers looking for demanding ascents and longer distances.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mula?
A: The Mula region is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers cooler conditions, suitable for longer excursions, though some higher mountain paths might be colder.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes near Mula?
A: Yes, the Mula area features beautiful natural water features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14623717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cierva Reservoir – Fuente Caputa Waterfall loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the scenic Fuente Caputa Waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another notable highlight in the vicinity.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I explore while hiking in Mula?
A: Mula's trails lead to several impressive natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can hike to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeces&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/704570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de Alhama Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The region also features interesting geological formations, including various caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4999129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Mataos&lt;/a&gt; and unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6496225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Formations with Caves&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find historical sites or cultural points of interest on Mula's trails?
A: Yes, Mula's hiking routes often pass through areas rich in history and culture. The Ricote Valley, for instance, features historic towns and fertile orchards. Some trails, like the Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail, offer glimpses into the region's past, including medieval castle ruins and archaeological sites.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking routes in Mula?
A: Absolutely. For families, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897791108/fuente-caputa-and-rambla-de-perea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa and Rambla de Perea Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's an easy 6.1 km trail that takes less than two hours to complete, offering scenic views and accessible paths suitable for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Mula?
A: Yes, many of Mula's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897791108/fuente-caputa-and-rambla-de-perea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa and Rambla de Perea Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21849337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through pine trees – Kilometer Marker MC 3 loop from Casas Nuevas&lt;/a&gt;. The region research also mentions challenging circular routes up to Pico de los Almeces.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Mula?
A: The hiking routes in Mula are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil valleys to challenging mountain paths, and the well-maintained trails that offer options for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging or long-distance hikes in the Mula region?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Mula offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38246387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Espuña North-facing Slope – Trail through pine trees loop from Casas Nuevas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18 km hike. Region research also highlights challenging routes like 'The summit of Almeces — Circular via the Pozo ravine' (17.4 km with 730m elevation gain) and 'The Sierra de Ricote and the Navela — Circular via the Collado de la Madera Trail' (16.2 km).

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Mula?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Mula region. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially in protected natural areas or private lands, and to keep your dog on a leash for their safety and to respect wildlife.
tips: Very quiet and picturesque trail that runs through a not so well-known area of the Sierra Espuña regional park. You can follow it from the town of Casas Nuevas and you will end up surrounding the Morrón de la Cabra. A very attractive walk through forests and beautiful landscapes.

faqs.question: Where does the Return to Morrón de la Cabra — Circular by La Umbría del Bosque (PR-MU 78) trail start?
faqs.answer: The PR-MU 78 trail officially begins in the village of Casas Nuevas, which is situated at an altitude of 500 meters in the northern part of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. This village also offers traditional accommodation and restaurants for hikers.

faqs.question: Is the PR-MU 78 trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The PR-MU 78 is categorized as a moderate hiking trail. While generally accessible, some sections can be deteriorated by runoff, fallen vegetation, and trees. It's best suited for hikers with some experience, and families with older children who are accustomed to longer walks and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers a remarkably diverse experience. You'll encounter rugged mountains, traditional cultivated areas, impressive rock formations like Piedra del Almirez and Morrón de la Cabra, and ancient drylands. The ascent provides spectacular panoramic views, while the descent leads through lush riverside vegetation along the Rambla de Malvariche. The trail also winds through deep pine forests, particularly within the Escarpadas hills of Perona and El Bosque, and offers views of olive groves and fields.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the PR-MU 78?
faqs.answer: Given the diverse landscape that includes both arid zones and lush areas, spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot in this region, and some sections might be affected by winter rains.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Return to Morrón de la Cabra — Circular by La Umbría del Bosque (PR-MU 78) trail?
faqs.answer: As the PR-MU 78 is located within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times. This is to protect the local wildlife, especially birds of prey and red-billed choughs, and to ensure the safety and enjoyment of all trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra Espuña Regional Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits required to hike the PR-MU 78 trail, nor is there an entrance fee for the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and taking all litter with them.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several striking features. You'll pass between impressive rock formations like Piedra del Almirez and the Morrón de la Cabra. The Cuesta de la Mina provides spectacular panoramic views, featuring sheer rock faces such as Perona and Piedras Blancas. You'll also experience the unique contrast of arid landscapes giving way to lush riverside vegetation along the Rambla de Malvariche. Don't miss the deep pine forests, especially in the Escarpadas hills of Perona and El Bosque, and the views of the Sierra Espuña North-facing Slope.

faqs.question: Does the PR-MU 78 trail intersect with other hiking or cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PR-MU 78 shares sections with several other official routes. You'll find it intersecting with parts of the Esputrek. Tramo 1 El Berro - Casas Nuevas, Espubike. Tramo 2 Gebas - Casas Nuevas, and the Camino de la Cruz del Bajo Guadalentín, among others. This offers opportunities to explore extended routes if desired.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The Return to Morrón de la Cabra — Circular by La Umbría del Bosque (PR-MU 78) is approximately 15.5 kilometers (11 miles) long. On average, hikers can expect to complete the route in about 4 to 5 hours, depending on their pace and the time spent enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see along the PR-MU 78 trail?
faqs.answer: The diverse habitats along the trail support various wildlife. Keep an eye out for birds of prey soaring above the sheer rock faces of Perona and Piedras Blancas. Red-billed choughs are also commonly spotted in these mountainous areas. The lush riverside sections may offer glimpses of other smaller animals and a variety of bird species.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Casas Nuevas?
faqs.answer: Yes, the village of Casas Nuevas, where the trail starts and ends, offers traditional accommodation options and local restaurants. These can be a great place to refuel and relax before or after your hike.
